title: Secrets in the Rafters
synopsis: When Mochi the shiba inu puppy gets trapped in the school attic during a storm, she discovers mysterious clues about a missing lunch box and must solve the puzzle on her own while making an unexpected friend.

pages.text:
Rain drums against the window like tiny paws on glass, and I notice something the teachers missed. A lunch box sits behind the old trunk, covered in dust that looks three days old. My nose tells me it smells like peanut butter and sadness.

I'm Mochi, and I notice things others don't. Right now I notice I'm stuck in the attic until someone remembers I followed Mrs. Chen up here to help carry supplies. The storm started while we worked, and she rushed downstairs when the lights flickered. The door clicked shut behind her.

My ears perk up at every creak in the wooden beams above. This attic stretches longer than I first thought, with shadows hiding in corners where the afternoon light can't reach. Dust motes dance in the gray light like tiny mysteries waiting to be solved.

illustration.description: A round chubby shiba inu puppy with cream-and-tan fur, perky triangular ears, a curly tail, and smiling brown eyes sniffs a dusty lunch box behind an old trunk in a long attic. Rain patters against a window as wooden beams stretch overhead. The illustration uses treasure map style with aged parchment colors, dotted trails leading to the lunch box, and compass roses marking corners.
illustration.alt: Mochi discovering a mysterious lunch box in the dusty attic

pages.text:
I pad closer to investigate, my paws silent on the creaky floorboards. The lunch box has a name written in fading marker: 'SANTIAGO.' But that's not what catches my attention. There are small wet drops next to it, too fresh for this dusty place.

My keen nose picks up something else - the scent trail of another animal. Not a dog like me, but something smaller. Something that might have been crying. The drops lead away from the lunch box toward a pile of old gymnasium mats stacked against the far wall.

The rain grows louder, and I realize no one will come looking for me until the storm stops. This mystery is mine to solve alone.

illustration.description: A round chubby shiba inu puppy with cream-and-tan fur, perky triangular ears, a curly tail, and smiling brown eyes follows a dotted trail of wet drops across dusty floorboards toward gymnasium mats. The lunch box labeled 'SANTIAGO' sits in the background. The scene is rendered in treasure map style with sepia tones and adventure cartography details.
illustration.alt: Mochi following a trail of tears across the attic floor

pages.text:
I return to the lunch box area, but this time I notice what I missed before. Tiny claw marks score the wooden trunk's edge - too small for my paws. And there's something else: a piece of orange fur caught on a splinter.

My investigation leads me back to the gymnasium mats, where I now detect the sound of quiet sniffling coming from underneath. Someone else is hiding in this attic, someone much smaller than me. My tail wags slightly because I recognize the scent now - it belongs to a cat.

'I know you're there,' I whisper toward the mats. 'I found Santiago's lunch box.'

A tiny orange face appears, then disappears quickly. But I saw enough - whiskers twitching with worry, green eyes wide with surprise. This little cat has been crying, and somehow they're connected to the missing lunch box mystery.

illustration.description: A round chubby shiba inu puppy with cream-and-tan fur, perky triangular ears, a curly tail, and smiling brown eyes peers at gymnasium mats where a small orange kitten's face peeks out. Orange fur clings to wooden splinters nearby. The attic scene is drawn in treasure map style with hand-drawn map details, jewel accents highlighting the clues, and aged parchment colors.
illustration.alt: Mochi discovering a hiding orange kitten among the gymnasium mats

pages.text:
The storm outside grows fiercer, rattling the small attic window. I settle down a respectful distance from the mats and wait. Patience is something we dogs understand, and right now this scared cat needs space to trust me.

'I won't hurt you,' I say quietly. 'But I'm curious about Santiago's lunch box. Do you know why it's been up here for three days?'

Slowly, the orange face emerges again. This time I see it's a kitten, much younger than me, with bright green eyes that shimmer with unshed tears. They're wearing a tiny collar with a bell that barely makes a sound.

'I took it,' the kitten whispers. 'I was so hungry, and it smelled so good. But then I couldn't get back down, and Santiago probably thinks someone stole his lunch.'

illustration.description: A round chubby shiba inu puppy with cream-and-tan fur, perky triangular ears, a curly tail, and smiling brown eyes sits patiently while a small orange kitten with green eyes and a tiny bell collar emerges from behind gymnasium mats. Rain streams down the window in the background. The illustration uses treasure map style with bold explorers theme, sepia tones, and compass rose details.
illustration.alt: Mochi patiently waiting as the orange kitten reveals themselves

pages.text:
Now I understand the whole mystery. I approach the lunch box again, but this time I'm not investigating - I'm planning. The kitten, who tells me their name is Paprika, has been trapped up here just like me, but for much longer.

'Santiago brings extra food every day,' I tell Paprika, remembering how I've seen the boy in the cafeteria. 'His grandmother packs too much because she loves him. He always shares with friends who forget their lunch.'

Paprika's ears perk up with interest. 'Really?'

'Really. But we need to get this back to him and explain what happened. I have an idea, but I'll need your help.'

Together, we examine the attic space with fresh eyes. I notice the heating vent near the floor has loose screws - probably how Paprika got up here in the first place. And I notice my keen hearing can detect voices from the classroom below.

illustration.description: A round chubby shiba inu puppy with cream-and-tan fur, perky triangular ears, a curly tail, and smiling brown eyes stands next to a small orange kitten with green eyes near a lunch box and loose heating vent. Both animals look determined as they plan together. The scene is drawn in treasure map style with dotted trails connecting clues, adventure cartography details, and jewel accents.
illustration.alt: Mochi and Paprika working together to solve their problem

pages.text:
The rain finally stops, and I hear footsteps on the stairs. 'Mrs. Chen!' I bark loudly. 'We're up here!'

When the door opens, Mrs. Chen's surprised face appears. 'Mochi! And... oh my, a kitten! How did you both get up here?'

Paprika mews softly and nudges Santiago's lunch box with their tiny paw. I wag my tail and look between the lunch box and Paprika, hoping Mrs. Chen will understand our signal.

'Is this Santiago's missing lunch box?' Mrs. Chen asks, picking it up. 'And little kitten, are you the one who's been living in our school?'

As we're carried downstairs, I catch Paprika's eye. We solved this mystery together, a dog and a cat who both notice things others miss. Sometimes the best friendships start with understanding someone else's hunger.

illustration.description: A round chubby shiba inu puppy with cream-and-tan fur, perky triangular ears, a curly tail, and smiling brown eyes sits next to a small orange kitten as Mrs. Chen holds Santiago's lunch box. Sunlight streams through the attic window as the storm clears. The illustration uses treasure map style with aged parchment colors, compass roses showing their successful journey, and bold explorers theme celebrating their friendship.
illustration.alt: Mochi and Paprika being rescued as their mystery is solved

metadata.lessonFocus: Solving problems independently while helping others

coverPrompt.description: A round chubby shiba inu puppy with cream-and-tan fur, perky triangular ears, a curly tail, and smiling brown eyes sits in a dusty attic with wooden beams overhead. Rain patters against a small window as golden light filters through, illuminating treasure map-style swirls of dust in the air. The illustration is done in treasure map style with aged parchment colors, compass roses in the corners, and sepia tones with jewel accents.
coverPrompt.alt: Mochi the shiba inu puppy exploring a mysterious attic during a rainstorm

questions.question: What does Mochi first notice about the lunch box?
questions.explanation: Mochi's sharp senses notice the dust on the lunch box, which tells her it's been there for three days.

questions.question: How does Mochi know another animal is in the attic?
questions.explanation: Mochi's excellent nose picks up the scent trail that leads her to discover Paprika.

questions.question: Why did Paprika take the lunch box?
questions.explanation: Paprika explains that they took the lunch box because they were hungry and it smelled good.

questions.question: What does Mochi tell Paprika about Santiago?
questions.explanation: Mochi knows that Santiago shares his extra food with friends who need it, showing his kind nature.

questions.question: How do Mochi and Paprika work together?
choices: They signal about the lunch box
questions.explanation: When Mrs. Chen arrives, both animals work together to show her the lunch box and help her understand what happened.
